Title: Successful User Registration with Valid Data
Preconditions: User is not already registered in the system.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., user@example.com), strong password (e.g., P@ssw0rd!), full name (e.g., John Doe), address (e.g., Via Roma 1, 30175 Venezia), phone number (e.g., +393123456789)
Test Steps:
1. Open the registration form.
2. Enter valid email address.
3. Enter strong password.
4. Confirm password.
5. Enter full name.
6. Enter address.
7. Enter phone number.
8. Select at least one checkbox for user type (e.g., "Anziano").
9. Click "Register" button.
Expected Result: User account is created successfully, and a confirmation message is displayed. User is redirected to the login screen.

Title: Registration with Weak Password
Preconditions: User is not already registered in the system.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., user@example.com), weak password (e.g., password), full name (e.g., John Doe), address (e.g., Via Roma 1, 30175 Venezia), phone number (e.g., +393123456789)
Test Steps:
1. Open the registration form.
2. Enter valid email address.
3. Enter weak password.
4. Confirm password.
5. Enter full name.
6. Enter address.
7. Enter phone number.
8. Select at least one checkbox for user type.
9. Click "Register" button.
Expected Result: Registration fails, and an error message is displayed: "Password must be strong (minimum 8 characters, including uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character)."

Title: Registration with Duplicate Email
Preconditions: A user with email "user@example.com" is already registered.
Test Data: Duplicate email (e.g., user@example.com), strong password (e.g., P@ssw0rd!), full name (e.g., John Doe), address (e.g., Via Roma 1, 30175 Venezia), phone number (e.g., +393123456789)
Test Steps:
1. Open the registration form.
2. Enter duplicate email address.
3. Enter strong password.
4. Confirm password.
5. Enter full name.
6. Enter address.
7. Enter phone number.
8. Select at least one checkbox for user type.
9. Click "Register" button.
Expected Result: Registration fails, and an error message is displayed: "Email already registered."

Title: Successful Login with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: User is registered in the system.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., user@example.com), correct password (e.g., P@ssw0rd!)
Test Steps:
1. Open the login screen.
2. Enter valid email address.
3. Enter correct password.
4. Click "Login" button.
Expected Result: User is logged in successfully, and the personal dashboard is displayed.

Title: Login with Invalid Password
Preconditions: User is registered in the system.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., user@example.com), incorrect password (e.g., wrongpass)
Test Steps:
1. Open the login screen.
2. Enter valid email address.
3. Enter incorrect password.
4. Click "Login" button.
Expected Result: Login fails, and an error message is displayed: "Invalid email or password."

Title: Login with Non-Existent Email
Preconditions: None.
Test Data: Non-existent email (e.g., nonexistent@example.com), any password (e.g., P@ssw0rd!)
Test Steps:
1. Open the login screen.
2. Enter non-existent email address.
3. Enter any password.
4. Click "Login" button.
Expected Result: Login fails, and an error message is displayed: "Invalid email or password."

Title: Publish a New Offer on Dashboard
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Data: Offer title (e.g., "Libri usati"), description (e.g., "Offro libri di seconda mano in buono stato"), type (e.g., "Baratto"), category (e.g., "Libri"), quantity (e.g., 5), location (e.g., "Via Roma 1, Venezia")
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the system.
2. Navigate to the personal dashboard.
3. Click on "Pubblica un'offerta" button.
4. Enter offer title.
5. Enter description.
6. Select offer type (e.g., "Baratto").
7. Select category (e.g., "Libri").
8. Enter quantity.
9. Enter location.
10. Click "Pubblica" button.
Expected Result: Offer is published successfully, and a confirmation message is displayed. The new offer appears in the "Offerte Pubblicate" section of the dashboard.

Title: Search Offers by Keyword
Preconditions: At least one offer is published in the system.
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the system.
2. Navigate to the "Esplora Offerte" section.
3. Enter a keyword in the search bar (e.g., "cibo").
4. Click the search button.
Expected Result: A list of offers matching the keyword is displayed.

Title: Propose an Exchange for an Offer
Preconditions: User is logged in, and at least one offer is published by another user.
Test Data: Offer ID (e.g., 101), exchange description (e.g., "Offro un libro in cambio di cibo")
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the system.
2. Navigate to the "Esplora Offerte" section.
3. Select an offer to propose an exchange.
4. Enter exchange description.
5. Click "Proponi Scambio" button.
Expected Result: Exchange proposal is sent successfully, and a confirmation message is displayed. The proposal appears in the "Proposte di Scambio" section of the offer owner’s dashboard.

Title: Propose Exchange for Own Offer
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one published offer.
Test Data: Own offer ID (e.g., 101), exchange description (e.g., "Offro un libro in cambio di cibo")
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the system.
2. Navigate to the "Esplora Offerte" section.
3. Select the user’s own published offer.
4. Attempt to propose an exchange for the own offer.
Expected Result: Exchange proposal fails, and an error message is displayed: "Non puoi proporre uno scambio per la tua offerta."

Title: Successful User Registration
Preconditions: User has installed the app and possesses a personal email address.
Test Data: Valid name, email, password, and confirmation.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application for the first time.
2. User selects the registration option.
3. User fills in the form with valid personal data (name, email, password, confirmation).
4. User submits the form.
Expected Result: System validates the data, adds the user to the database, and enables access to the application.

Title: User Already Registered Attempts Registration
Preconditions: User has previously registered and installed the app.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application.
2. User attempts to register again with existing credentials.
Expected Result: System detects prior registration and redirects the user to the login screen without creating a duplicate account.

Title: Successful Login with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: User has a valid registered account.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application.
2. User enters valid credentials (email/password).
3. User submits the login form.
Expected Result: System validates credentials, logs the user in, and grants access to the application.

Title: Login with Invalid Credentials
Preconditions: User has an account but is not logged in.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application.
2. User enters invalid credentials (wrong password or non-registered email).
3. User submits the login form.
Expected Result: System rejects the login attempt and displays an error message (e.g., "Invalid credentials").

Title: Publish New Item to Bacheca
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Data: Valid item details (name, description, category, etc.).
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the "Add New Item" button.
2. User fills in the form with valid item details.
3. User submits the form.
Expected Result: System validates the data, adds the item to the database, and displays it in the user’s bacheca.

Title: Explore Offers from Other Users
Preconditions: User is registered and logged in.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User taps the icon to view other users' offers.
Expected Result: The system displays a list of offers from other users. If no offers exist, a blank screen is shown.

Title: Apply Filters to Search Offers
Preconditions: User is registered and logged in.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User taps the icon to view other users' offers.
3. User taps the filter icon to open the filter menu.
4. User selects filters: Location = "City X", Type = "Objects", Purpose = "Barter".
5. User applies the filters.
Expected Result: The system displays only offers matching the selected filters (location, type, purpose).

Title: Propose Exchange for Free Item
Preconditions: User is registered, logged in, and viewing another user’s offer for a free item.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User navigates to another user’s offer for a free item.
3. User taps the "Propose Exchange" button.
Expected Result: The system allows the user to propose an exchange without requiring additional items in their bacheca.

Title: Propose Exchange for Paid Item
Preconditions: User is registered, logged in, viewing another user’s offer for a non-free item, and has at least one item in their bacheca.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User navigates to another user’s offer for a non-free item.
3. User taps the "Propose Exchange" button.
4. User selects an item from their bacheca to exchange.
Expected Result: The system sends a barter request to the other user with the selected item.

Title: View Active Exchange Proposals
Preconditions: User has at least one active exchange proposal (sent or received).
Test Data: User has 2 active proposals (1 sent, 1 received).
Test Steps: 1. User navigates to the "Active Exchange Proposals" screen.
Expected Result: The system displays a list of active proposals in descending chronological order, including details and status (e.g., accepted, rejected).

Title: View Empty Proposal List
Preconditions: User has no active exchange proposals.
Test Steps: 1. User navigates to the "Active Exchange Proposals" screen.
Expected Result: The system displays an empty screen indicating no active proposals.

Title: Accept an Exchange Proposal
Preconditions: User has items in their bacheca and has received an exchange proposal.
Test Data: User has 1 item in bacheca, 1 proposal received.
Test Steps:
1. User receives a notification about a new proposal.
2. User opens the proposal.
3. User taps the "Accept" button.
Expected Result: The system updates the proposal status to "accepted" and sends a notification to the offerer.

Title: Reject an Exchange Proposal
Preconditions: User has received an exchange proposal.
Test Steps:
1. User receives a notification about a new proposal.
2. User opens the proposal.
3. User taps the "Reject" button.
Expected Result: The system updates the proposal status to "rejected" and sends a notification to the offerer.

Title: Update Personal Data
Preconditions: User is registered and logged in.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User navigates to the "Personal Data" section.
3. User modifies the email field to "new@example.com".
4. User confirms the changes.
Expected Result: The system validates the new email, updates the database, and reflects the changes in the user profile.

Title: Validate email format during user registration
Preconditions: User opens registration form
Test Steps:
1. User enters invalid email in the registration form.
2. User submits the form.
Expected Result: System blocks registration and displays an error message about invalid email format.

Title: Check username availability during registration
Preconditions: User opens registration form
Test Data: Username already taken by another user
Test Steps:
1. User enters an existing username in the registration form.
2. User submits the form.
Expected Result: System blocks registration and displays an error message about username unavailability.

Title: Prevent duplicate email registration
Preconditions: User opens registration form
Test Data: Email already registered in the system
Test Steps:
1. User enters an email already used by another registered user.
2. User submits the form.
Expected Result: System blocks registration and displays an error message about duplicate email.

Title: Save new user data to database
Preconditions: User provides valid email, unique username, and password
Test Data: Valid and unique email, username, and password
Test Steps:
1. User enters valid and unique email, username, and password.
2. User submits the form.
Expected Result: System saves user data to the database and confirms successful registration.

Title: Validate login credentials
Preconditions: User opens login form
Test Steps:
1. User enters incorrect username/email and password.
2. User submits the form.
Expected Result: System rejects login and displays an error message.

Title: Grant access after successful login
Preconditions: User opens login form
Test Steps:
1. User enters correct username/email and password.
2. User submits the form.
Expected Result: System grants access to the application, displays user’s personal information, and enables authorized functionalities.

Title: Display user’s personal bacheca
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps: 1. User navigates to the personal bacheca section.
Expected Result: System displays all items previously published by the user.

Title: Provide form for new item publication
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps: 1. User clicks on the "Publish New Item" button.
Expected Result: System displays a form for inserting a new good/service.

Title: Validate new item data
Preconditions: User is logged in and opens the publication form
Test Data: Missing required fields (e.g., item name, description)
Test Steps: 1. User submits the form without filling all required fields.
Expected Result: System blocks submission and displays an error message about missing required data.

Title: Save new item to database
Preconditions: User is logged in and fills all required fields in the publication form
Test Steps: 1. User submits the form with valid data.
Expected Result: System saves the new item to the database and confirms successful publication.

Title: Confirm item deletion
Preconditions: User is logged in and selects an item for deletion
Test Data: Item not involved in any ongoing exchange
Test Steps:
1. User selects an item and clicks "Delete."
2. System displays a confirmation popup.
3. User confirms deletion.
Expected Result: System shows a confirmation message that the item has been deleted.

Title: Prevent deletion of item in ongoing exchange
Preconditions: User is logged in and selects an item involved in an ongoing exchange
Test Data: Item currently part of an active exchange
Test Steps: 1. User selects an item involved in an ongoing exchange and clicks "Delete."
Expected Result: System blocks deletion and displays an error message about the item being part of an active exchange.

Title: Remove item from database
Preconditions: User confirms deletion of an item not involved in any exchange
Test Data: Item not involved in any ongoing exchange
Test Steps: 1. User confirms deletion of an item.
Expected Result: System removes the item from the database and it is no longer visible in the application.

Title: Retrieve all published items from database
Preconditions: Items are published by users
Test Steps: 1. User navigates to the offers exploration section.
Expected Result: System retrieves and displays all published items from the database.

Title: Display list of available items for barter
Preconditions: Items are published by users
Test Steps: 1. User navigates to the offers exploration section.
Expected Result: System displays an updated list of all available items for barter published by other users.

Title: Initiate an Exchange Proposal
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one item/service listed for barter.
Test Data: Valid item/service ID available for exchange.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Exchange Proposal" section.
2. User selects an item/service from the available list.
3. User confirms the proposal submission.
Expected Result: The system allows the user to submit the exchange proposal successfully.

Title: Verify Item Availability Before Proposal
Preconditions: User is logged in and has an item/service listed.
Test Data: An item/service marked as "available" and another marked as "already bartered."
Test Steps:
1. User attempts to propose an exchange for an item/service marked as "available."
2. User attempts to propose an exchange for an item/service marked as "already bartered."
Expected Result: The system allows the proposal for the "available" item/service but blocks the proposal for the "already bartered" item/service with an error message.

Title: Display Complete List of Exchange Proposals
Preconditions: Multiple exchange proposals exist in the system.
Test Data: Three active exchange proposals (e.g., Proposal A, Proposal B, Proposal C).
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Exchange Proposals" section.
2. User views the list of proposals.
Expected Result: The system displays all three active proposals (A, B, C) in the list.

Title: Show Proposal Status
Preconditions: Multiple exchange proposals exist with different statuses.
Test Data: One accepted proposal, one rejected proposal, and one pending proposal.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Exchange Proposals" section.
2. User checks the status of each proposal in the list.
Expected Result: The system displays the status (accepted, rejected, pending) for each proposal.

Title: Remove Proposal After Exchange Completion
Preconditions: One active exchange proposal exists.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Exchange Proposals" section before the exchange is completed.
2. User confirms the exchange for Proposal D.
3. User refreshes the list of proposals.
Expected Result: The system removes Proposal D from the list after the exchange is completed, and only Proposal E (if still pending) remains visible.

Title: Accept an Exchange Offer
Preconditions: User has an active exchange proposal in their bacheca.
Test Data: Proposal F (offer for an item/service in user’s bacheca).
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to their bacheca.
2. User locates Proposal F and selects the "Accept" option.
3. User confirms the acceptance.
Expected Result: The system accepts Proposal F and updates its status to "accepted."

Title: Reject an Exchange Offer
Preconditions: User has an active exchange proposal in their bacheca.
Test Data: Proposal G (offer for an item/service in user’s bacheca).
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to their bacheca.
2. User locates Proposal G and selects the "Reject" option.
3. User confirms the rejection.
Expected Result: The system rejects Proposal G and updates its status to "rejected."

Title: Filter Searches by Location and Category
Preconditions: Multiple items/services are listed across different locations and categories.
Test Steps:
1. User applies a filter for location "Rome" and category "goods."
2. User applies a filter for location "Milan" and category "services."
Expected Result: The system displays only items/services matching the selected location and category (e.g., only goods in Rome for the first filter).

Title: Modify Personal Data
Preconditions: User is logged in and has existing personal data.
Test Data: Current email: old@example.com, new email: new@example.com (valid).
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Personal Data" section.
2. User updates the email field from old@example.com to new@example.com.
3. User submits the changes.
Expected Result: The system allows the update and reflects the new email in the user profile.

Title: Validate Updated Personal Data
Preconditions: User is logged in with existing personal data.
Test Data: Current email: old@example.com, new email: invalid-email (invalid).
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Personal Data" section.
2. User attempts to update the email to invalid-email.
3. User submits the changes.
Expected Result: The system rejects the update and displays an error message indicating the email is invalid. The old email remains unchanged.

Title: Verify Android Version Compatibility
Preconditions: None
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on a device running Android 8.0 (API 26).
2. Launch the application.
3. Navigate through all primary features (e.g., login, image upload, bacheca view).
Expected Result: The application launches and functions without crashes or errors.

Title: Verify API 27 Compatibility
Preconditions: None
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on a device running Android 8.0+ (targeting API 27).
2. Attempt to use all core functionalities (e.g., image upload, database sync).
Expected Result: The application works as expected without compatibility issues on devices supporting API 27.

Title: Verify Temporary Image Cache Clearing
Preconditions: The app has cached at least 5 images in the temporary storage.
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application and upload 5 images to the bacheca.
2. Close the application completely (force stop if necessary).
3. Reopen the application.
4. Check if the cached images are still present in the app storage.
Expected Result: The cached images are automatically deleted upon app closure.

Title: Verify Intuitive UI and Error Tolerance
Preconditions: None
Test Steps:
1. Open the application for the first time.
2. Attempt to perform a task (e.g., upload an image) with 3 consecutive incorrect inputs (e.g., wrong file format, wrong button clicks).
3. Complete the task correctly on the 4th attempt.
Expected Result: The application allows the user to complete the task successfully after 3 errors, without locking or crashing.

Title: Verify Auto-Cleanup on Uninstall
Preconditions: The application is installed and has cached data or user files.
Test Steps:
1. Install the application and perform actions that generate cached data (e.g., upload images, create posts).
2. Uninstall the application via device settings.
3. Check the device storage for leftover app data.
Expected Result: All cached data and user files are automatically deleted during uninstallation.

Title: Verify Password Storage Security
Preconditions: A user account with a password is created in the app.
Test Steps:
1. Register a new user with the provided credentials.
2. Use a database inspection tool (e.g., Firebase console) to check the stored password.
Expected Result: The password is stored as a hashed value with salt, not in plaintext.

Title: Verify HTTPS Communication
Preconditions: The device has an active internet connection.
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application and attempt to log in or sync data.
2. Use a packet sniffer (e.g., Wireshark) to monitor network traffic between the app and Firebase.
Expected Result: All communications use HTTPS (port 443) and are encrypted.

Title: Verify Database Response Time
Preconditions: The device has a stable internet connection.
Test Steps:
1. Perform an action requiring database sync (e.g., upload an image, fetch posts).
2. Measure the time taken for the app to receive a response from the database.
Expected Result: The response time does not exceed 3 seconds.

Title: Verify Crash Frequency
Preconditions: The application is installed and used for 7 consecutive days.
Test Steps:
1. Use the application daily for core functionalities (e.g., login, upload, view posts).
2. Monitor crashes using Android’s built-in crash reporting or a logging tool.
Expected Result: The application crashes no more than 3 times in a 7-day period.

Title: Verify Offline Behavior
Preconditions: The device has no active internet connection.
Test Steps:
1. Disable internet on the device.
2. Attempt to perform actions requiring online access (e.g., login, upload image, sync data).
3. Re-enable internet and retry the actions.
Expected Result: The app either shows an appropriate error message or queues actions for later execution. No data is lost upon reconnection.

Title: Verify Camera Functionality
Preconditions: The device has a functional camera.
Test Steps:
1. Open the image upload feature in the app.
2. Attempt to take a photo using the app’s camera.
3. Save the photo to the bacheca.
Expected Result: The camera launches successfully, captures the image, and the image is saved to the bacheca without errors.

Title: Verify Minimum Storage Requirement
Preconditions: The device has exactly 150 MB of free storage.
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to install the application on the device.
2. Launch the application and perform basic actions (e.g., open the main screen).
Expected Result: The application installs successfully and launches without storage-related errors.

Title: Verify Internet Dependency
Preconditions: The device has no active internet connection.
Test Steps:
1. Disable internet on the device.
2. Attempt to launch the application or perform any action.
Expected Result: The application either fails to launch or shows an error message indicating the need for an internet connection. No critical functionality works without internet.

Title: Validate uniqueness of username during registration
Preconditions: User is on the registration form
Test Steps:
1. User enters "testuser123" as username in the registration form.
2. User attempts to submit the form.
3. User enters "newuser456" as username in the registration form.
4. User attempts to submit the form.
Expected Result:
Step 1: System rejects submission with error message "Username already exists."
Step 2: System rejects submission with error message "Username already exists."
Step 3: System accepts submission and proceeds to next step.

Title: Validate uniqueness of email during registration
Preconditions: User is on the registration form
Test Steps:
1. User enters "test@example.com" as email in the registration form.
2. User attempts to submit the form.
3. User enters "newuser@example.com" as email in the registration form.
4. User attempts to submit the form.
Expected Result:
Step 1: System rejects submission with error message "Email already registered."
Step 2: System accepts submission and proceeds to next step.

Title: Complete user registration with valid data
Preconditions: User is on the registration form, all fields are filled with valid data
Test Data: Valid email "newuser@example.com", valid username "newuser456", valid name, valid surname, valid date of birth, valid location
Test Steps:
1. User fills all fields with valid data.
2. User submits the registration form.
Expected Result: System validates all data, confirms registration success, and saves user data to the database.

Title: Validate login with correct credentials
Preconditions: User is registered with username "testuser123" and password "password123"
Test Steps:
1. User enters "testuser123" as username and "password123" as password.
2. User submits the login form.
Expected Result: System validates credentials, grants access to the application, and displays user’s personal information.

Title: Validate login with incorrect credentials
Preconditions: User is registered with username "testuser123" and password "password123"
Test Steps:
1. User enters "testuser123" as username and "wrongpassword" as password.
2. User submits the login form.
Expected Result: System rejects login attempt with error message "Invalid username or password."

Title: Display user’s uploaded items in personal dashboard
Preconditions: User is logged in, user has uploaded at least one item to the dashboard
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User navigates to the personal dashboard.
Expected Result: System displays all items uploaded by the user in a clear and intuitive manner.

Title: Display form for uploading new item to dashboard
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User navigates to the personal dashboard.
3. User clicks on the option to upload a new item.
Expected Result: System displays a form for inserting a new item into the user’s dashboard.

Title: Validate mandatory fields in new item form
Preconditions: User has an account and is logged in
Test Data: Empty form fields (title, description, category), invalid email, missing price
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Inserimento bene/servizio" form
2. User clicks "Invia" without filling required fields
3. User enters invalid email format (e.g., "test@")
4. User leaves price field empty
5. User clicks "Invia" again
Expected Result: System displays validation errors for each missing/invalid field; form does not submit

Title: Validate data types in new item form
Preconditions: User has an account and is logged in
Test Data: Non-numeric price (e.g., "abc"), negative price (-10), special characters in title (e.g., "Item@")
Test Steps:
1. User enters "abc" in the price field
2. User enters "-10" in the price field
3. User enters "Item@" in the title field
4. User clicks "Invia"
Expected Result: System rejects submission and highlights invalid fields with appropriate error messages

Title: Publish valid item to personal bulletin board
Preconditions: User has filled and validated the form
Test Data: Valid item details (title, description, category, price, images)
Test Steps:
1. User fills all form fields with valid data
2. User clicks "Invia"
3. System validates data successfully
4. User confirms submission
Expected Result: Item is saved to database; user sees confirmation message; item appears in user’s bulletin board

Title: Display deletion confirmation popup
Preconditions: User has at least one item in their bulletin board
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to their bulletin board
2. User taps the "Elimina" button on an item
Expected Result:
System displays popup with:
Highlighted confirmation text (e.g., "Confermi l’eliminazione?")
"Annulla" button
"Conferma" button

Title: Block deletion of item in active exchange
Preconditions: User has an item involved in an ongoing exchange
Test Data: Item ID involved in an active exchange proposal
Test Steps:
1. User attempts to delete an item currently in an exchange proposal
2. User taps "Elimina" and confirms in popup
Expected Result: System prevents deletion; displays error message: "Impossibile eliminare: elemento coinvolto in scambio in corso"

Title: Permanently delete item from bulletin board
Preconditions: User has confirmed deletion of an item not in any exchange
Test Data: Item ID not involved in any exchange
Test Steps:
1. User taps "Elimina" on an item
2. User confirms in popup
Expected Result:
Item is removed from:
User’s bulletin board view
Database records
System prevents any user from viewing the deleted item

Title: Retrieve offers based on active filters
Preconditions: Database contains published offers
Test Steps:
1. User applies filters (category, location, price range)
2. User navigates to "Esplorazione delle offerte" screen
Expected Result: System retrieves and displays only offers matching all active filters

Title: Display offers in intuitive format
Preconditions: Database contains at least 3 published offers
Test Steps: 1. User navigates to "Esplorazione delle offerte" screen
Expected Result:
System displays:
Clear title for the section
List/grid of offers with:
Item images
Titles
Prices
Owner usernames
Intuitive navigation between pages if offers exceed one screen

Title: Initiate exchange proposal to another user
Preconditions: User is logged in and viewing another user’s bulletin board
Test Data: Valid item from target user’s board, valid item from proposer’s board
Test Steps:
1. User selects an item from another user’s bulletin board
2. User selects their own item to propose in exchange
3. User clicks "Proponi scambio"
Expected Result:
System:
Saves proposal to database
Notifies target user via in-app message
Prevents selection of items already in other exchanges

Title: Prevent proposal for already exchanged items
Preconditions: User has items in their board (some already exchanged)
Test Data: Item ID that was previously exchanged
Test Steps: 1. User attempts to propose an item already involved in a completed exchange
Expected Result:
System:
Greys out/removes the item from selection options
Displays tooltip: "Oggetto già scambiato" on hover
Prevents proposal submission for that item

Title: View All Swap Proposals for Logged-in User
Preconditions: User is logged in and in the swap proposals section.
Test Data: User account with at least one incoming and one outgoing swap proposal.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User navigates to the swap proposals section.
3. User views the list of proposals.
Expected Result: The system displays all active incoming and outgoing swap proposals for the user.

Title: Display Status of Outgoing Swap Proposals
Preconditions: User is logged in and in the swap proposals section.
Test Data: User account with outgoing proposals marked as accepted, rejected, or canceled.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User navigates to the swap proposals section.
3. User views the status of outgoing proposals.
Expected Result: The system shows the status (accepted, rejected, or canceled) of all outgoing proposals sent by the user.

Title: Remove Completed Swap Proposals from List
Preconditions: User is logged in and in the swap proposals section. At least one swap proposal is marked as completed.
Test Data: User account with one completed swap proposal.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User navigates to the swap proposals section.
3. System automatically removes the completed proposal from the list.
Expected Result: The user sees only active (non-completed) swap proposals in the list.

Title: Accept an Incoming Swap Proposal
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one active incoming swap proposal.
Test Data: One active incoming swap proposal from another user.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User navigates to the swap proposals section.
3. User selects an active incoming proposal.
4. User clicks "Accept" for the selected proposal.
Expected Result: The proposal is marked as accepted in the database, and both users can proceed with the physical barter.

Title: Reject an Incoming Swap Proposal
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one active incoming swap proposal.
Test Data: One active incoming swap proposal from another user.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User navigates to the swap proposals section.
3. User selects an active incoming proposal.
4. User clicks "Reject" for the selected proposal.
Expected Result: The proposal is marked as rejected in the database, and the proposing user can reuse the goods/services for other proposals.

Title: Filter Offers by Category and Location
Preconditions: User is logged in and in the offers exploration section.
Test Data: Multiple offers categorized as goods, barterable services, and donatable items, spread across different locations.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User navigates to the offers exploration section.
3. User selects filters: e.g., "goods" category and a specific location.
4. User applies the filters.
Expected Result: The system displays only offers matching the selected category and location.

Title: Update User Personal Data
Preconditions: User is logged in with a valid account.
Test Data: Updated personal data (e.g., name, email, or other account details).
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User navigates to the personal data section.
3. User modifies one or more personal details.
4. User submits the changes.
Expected Result: The system updates the user’s data in the database and reflects the changes in the profile display.

Title: Verify Android OS Compatibility
Preconditions: None
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on a device running Android 7.1 Nougat.
2. Attempt to launch the application.
3. Observe the behavior of the application.
Expected Result: The application should fail to install or launch, displaying an error message indicating the minimum required OS version (Android 8.0 Oreo or higher).

Title: Verify Camera Functionality Availability
Preconditions: None
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on a device without a camera.
2. Attempt to access any feature requiring camera functionality.
3. Observe the behavior of the application.
Expected Result: The application should fail to access camera-related features, displaying an error message indicating the absence of a camera.

Title: Verify Firebase Realtime Database Integration
Preconditions: Firebase Realtime Database is configured and accessible.
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application on a device with internet connectivity.
2. Perform an action that triggers data read/write operations (e.g., login, data submission).
3. Monitor the Firebase Realtime Database for real-time updates.
Expected Result: The application should successfully interact with Firebase Realtime Database, and data should reflect real-time updates without conflicts in read/write operations.

Title: Verify Database Conflict Avoidance
Preconditions: Firebase Realtime Database is configured with concurrent access from multiple devices.
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application on two different devices simultaneously.
2. Perform conflicting write operations (e.g., editing the same data entry) on both devices.
3. Observe the behavior of the application and the database.
Expected Result: The application should handle conflicting write operations gracefully, either by merging changes or notifying the user of conflicts, ensuring data consistency in Firebase Realtime Database.
